mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending consumer spending contraction, which triggers equity market sell-offs and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and producing positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold strategy by more than 100 bps over 60 days, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rebound in the index of industrial production following a contraction phase indicates capacity utilization recovery, generating short-term momentum and positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in 70% of post-contraction rebound months, or the signal exhibits negative autocorrelation in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a stagnant federal funds rate compress real interest rates, eroding corporate profit margins and triggering negative equity returns as markets price in stagflation risks.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.0 and FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%, or the interaction effect reverses sign in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting wage growth and consumer confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in over 60% of observations, or the correlation between the interaction term and forward returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below 1.5% quarter-over-quarter, it indicates insufficient aggregate demand to sustain earnings growth, leading to equity valuation compression and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 1.5%, or the signal's information coefficient remains negative across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expanding faster than consumer price inflation signals improving corporate operating leverage and margin expansion, generating positive equity returns during periods of stable economic growth.
failure_condition: The margin expansion signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when inflation accelerates, or the interaction term shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward tests.
